problem: Bump helix-cli package version from 1.3.2 to 1.3.3 in preparation for the next npm release. The publish workflow validates that the Git tag version matches package.json version, so package.json must be updated before a v1.3.3 tag is pushed.

files.why: Primary version source. Line 3: "version": "1.3.2". Must be changed to "1.3.3". Also defines build/test scripts (build=tsc, typecheck=tsc --noEmit, test=tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js, prepare=npm run build), npm publish config (public access, provenance), and files field ([dist, !tests, skill-content]).
Contains version "1.3.2" on lines 3 and 9. Must stay in sync with package.json. Updated automatically by npm when package.json version changes.

Publish workflow triggered by v* tags. Lines 30-38 validate that the tag version matches package.json version — if they diverge, the workflow exits with an error. No changes needed in this file; it reads package.json dynamically.
Runtime version reader. Reads version from package.json at runtime via getPackageVersion(). No hardcoded version string. No changes needed.
TypeScript config: target ES2022, module Node16, outDir dist, rootDir src. Needed for typecheck/build validation after the bump.
Excludes test files (dist/**/*.test.js, dist/**/*.test.d.ts) from npm tarball. No version reference. No changes needed.

facts: package.json line 3 shows "version": "1.3.2" — confirmed by direct read.
package-lock.json lines 3 and 9 both show "version": "1.3.2" — must be updated in sync.

The publish workflow (.github/workflows/publish.yml) triggers on v* tag push and validates tag version matches package.json version (lines 30-38). It then runs npm ci, npm test, npm pack with tarball validation, and npm publish --provenance.
The CLI reads its version dynamically from package.json at runtime via src/update/version.ts — no hardcoded version string exists in source code.
No CHANGELOG, README, or other release metadata files exist in the repo. No additional metadata files need updating.
No version string '1.3.2' appears in any source (.ts) file — grep confirmed. The version references in source are dynamic only.
skill-content/ files (SKILL.md, references/commands.md) reference --version as a command but do not contain a hardcoded version number.
Build scripts: build=tsc, typecheck=tsc --noEmit, test=tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js, prepare=npm run build.
Package name is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli, published publicly to npm registry with provenance.
No ORM, no migrations, no database schema files in this repo.
No CI workflow files besides publish.yml exist.
unknowns: Whether npm ci or npm install should be used to regenerate package-lock.json after the version bump, or if a manual edit of both files suffices. Both approaches produce a valid lockfile, but npm install is the standard method.
Whether the repo has any downstream consumers that pin to a specific version and might need notification — out of scope for this ticket but noted.
